title: Understanding Torque
text: Torque is the rotational equivalent of force. It is calculated as the product of the moment of inertia and angular acceleration.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Sphere
text: The moment of inertia (I) for a uniform solid sphere is given by the formula $$I=\frac{2}{5} M R^2$$, where M is the mass and R is the radius of the sphere.

title: Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration can be found by dividing the change in angular velocity by the time over which the change occurs. Here, the change in angular velocity is from $$0$$ to $$1$$ $$\frac{rev}{day}$$ over $$6$$ days.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Using the formula $$I=\frac{2}{5} M R^2$$, calculate the moment of inertia of the Earth.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Convert $$1$$ $$\frac{rev}{day}$$ to radians per second and calculate the angular acceleration over $$6$$ days.

title: Calculate Torque
text: Using the formula $$Torque=I angular$$ acceleration, calculate the torque applied to the Earth.
